default_binding.THRESHOLD: the shut funfair on the pier
default_binding.ENTRANCE: the loose plank in the perimeter fence
default_binding.OPENING_MOMENT: a moonlit night at low tide
default_binding.DEADLINE: the tide turning and creeping back in
default_binding.DEADLINE_SIGN: cold water rising over the sand
default_binding.KEEPSAKE: Gran's brass key ring with its rose charm
default_binding.ROUTE_BW: the boardwalk steps to the sand
default_binding.ROUTE_OL: the stairs up to the loft
default_binding.TB_SMELL: old paper, brass polish, and peppermints
default_binding.TB_OLD_HINT: my measures stay with the herd
default_binding.HM_SECRET_NOTE: the mare keeps her own secrets
default_binding.HM_ROOF_TEAR: a torn flap of roof canvas
default_binding.WS_CHISEL: a rose chisel worn to fit a hand
default_binding.CV_CHIMES: wind chimes made of old brass rings
default_binding.OL_ROLL_LABEL: for the mare who keeps my measures
default_binding.OL_CREST_FIND: wiring, dust, and an abandoned nest
default_binding.OL_TRACING: a tracing of the saddle rose seam
default_binding.BW_UNDER: a barnacled forest of timber legs

slots.meaning: The child protagonist.
slots.guidance: A single kid-relatable explorer (name or role); capable, curious, not reckless.

slots.meaning: The hero's deceased grandmother, the maker whose legacy drives the mystery.
slots.guidance: A beloved relative-maker, gone a year; referenced with warmth, never as a source of fear.

slots.meaning: The ancestor's formal/credit name, used on stamps, ledgers, and labels.
slots.guidance: A short formal or initialed name distinct from the hero's and the guardian's.

slots.meaning: The ancestor's craft or trade.
slots.guidance: A single benign skilled trade (e.g. carver, weaver, glassblower).

slots.meaning: The ancestor's short handwritten motto, reused as one ending's title.
slots.guidance: A warm, short maker's-motto phrase; reads naturally as a title on its own.

slots.meaning: The site's caretaker, a secondary helper figure who loves the place.
slots.guidance: An older, kind caretaker figure; never a threat; quietly devoted to the monument.

slots.meaning: The caretaker's name, as it appears on his door sign.
slots.guidance: A short formal name distinct from the hero's and the ancestor's.

slots.meaning: The whole bounded, explorable place the story happens inside.
slots.guidance: One shut site with several distinct rooms/zones and one entrance; wonder over dread; no toxic air, no lethal hazards.

slots.meaning: The single entry/exit point the hero uses to slip in and out.
slots.guidance: A small, known way in (a gap, a loose board); always reachable and safe to reach.

slots.meaning: The recurring, non-lethal condition that makes tonight's visit possible.
slots.guidance: A repeatable, safe night-time window; must not read as a one-off.

slots.meaning: The non-lethal time pressure that will eventually close the visit.
slots.guidance: A survivable natural deadline; being caught by it means an inconvenient retreat, never harm.

slots.meaning: The sensory sign the deadline is arriving.
slots.guidance: A visible, gradual cue that gives fair warning to retreat safely.

slots.meaning: The endangered structure at the heart of the mystery (a short name).
slots.guidance: A short 2-4 word name for the beloved threatened landmark; used in beats and ending titles alike.

slots.meaning: The specific structural damage threatening the monument.
slots.guidance: A short, named structural weakness that reads as fixable, never as an imminent collapse hazard.

slots.meaning: The inherited object the hero carries all night.
slots.guidance: A small carried keepsake from the ancestor, safe to hold and use gently.

slots.meaning: The ancestor's recurring carved/painted signature motif.
slots.guidance: A small recurring decorative signature mark, found in several places.

slots.meaning: The general animal/figure type the monument's carved figures depict.
slots.guidance: A single benign animal or figure type (e.g. horse, swan, dragon).

slots.meaning: The generic descriptor for the monument's single lead/star figure.
slots.guidance: A short descriptive phrase naming the standout figure (not its proper name).

slots.meaning: The lead figure's proper given name.
slots.guidance: A single proper name, distinct from every character's name in this contract.

slots.meaning: The hidden object the hero is ultimately searching for.
slots.guidance: The ancestor's hidden repair plans/instructions; a benign paper object.

slots.meaning: The ancestor's one-line community-repair philosophy, written on the plans.
slots.guidance: A short, warm line about many hands sharing the work.

slots.meaning: Ending: the full title naming the monument's restoration (success).
slots.guidance: A short, positive whole-title phrase naming the monument coming back to life.

slots.meaning: The identity of one of the six rooms/zones off the central hub.
slots.guidance: A short, distinct named room or zone within the threshold; must read as clearly different from its five siblings.

slots.meaning: The identity of one of the six rooms/zones off the central hub.
slots.guidance: A short, distinct named room or zone within the threshold; must read as clearly different from its five siblings.

slots.meaning: The identity of one of the six rooms/zones off the central hub.
slots.guidance: A short, distinct named room or zone within the threshold; must read as clearly different from its five siblings.

slots.meaning: The identity of one of the six rooms/zones off the central hub.
slots.guidance: A short, distinct named room or zone within the threshold; must read as clearly different from its five siblings.

slots.meaning: The identity of one of the six rooms/zones off the central hub.
slots.guidance: A short, distinct named room or zone within the threshold; must read as clearly different from its five siblings.

slots.meaning: The identity of one of the six rooms/zones off the central hub.
slots.guidance: A short, distinct named room or zone within the threshold; must read as clearly different from its five siblings.

slots.meaning: The distinctive smell inside the ticket-booth-equivalent room.
slots.guidance: A short benign smell descriptor.

slots.meaning: The founding year stamped in the oldest ledger.
slots.guidance: A single 4-digit-style year token.

slots.meaning: The ancestor's handwritten margin clue in the old ledger.
slots.guidance: A short cryptic-but-benign handwritten clue phrase.

slots.meaning: The painted decoration on the mirror-room door.
slots.guidance: A short whimsical painted figure/motif.

slots.meaning: The harmless prop that startles the hero in the tall mirrors.
slots.guidance: A short benign startle prop, clearly harmless once seen.

slots.meaning: The second set of initials found chalked beside the ancestor's.
slots.guidance: A short two-letter initials token.

slots.meaning: The short chalk note found beneath the poster.
slots.guidance: A short cryptic, benign note phrase.

slots.meaning: The torn roof material that causes the flicker.
slots.guidance: A short benign roof/covering-tear descriptor.

slots.meaning: The workshop's characteristic smell.
slots.guidance: A short benign craft-material smell descriptor.

slots.meaning: The small hand tool sized just for the hero.
slots.guidance: A short benign hand tool descriptor sized for a child.

slots.meaning: The short handwritten clue found in the workshop bench drawer.
slots.guidance: A short cryptic-but-benign clue phrase.

slots.meaning: The wind chimes outside the caretaker's home.
slots.guidance: A short benign wind-chime descriptor, ideally echoing the monument's material.

slots.meaning: The keepsakes covering the caretaker's table.
slots.guidance: A short benign collection of keepsakes/mementos.

slots.meaning: The instrument/mechanism housed in the loft room.
slots.guidance: A short name for a benign mechanical or musical fixture.

slots.meaning: The old printed program the hero finds.
slots.guidance: A short benign printed-keepsake descriptor.

slots.meaning: The short label on the heaviest music/record roll.
slots.guidance: A short handwritten dedication phrase.

slots.meaning: The harmless contents found behind the crest panel.
slots.guidance: A short benign, slightly funny dead-end find.

slots.meaning: The tracing paper diagram found with the roll.
slots.guidance: A short benign technical-drawing descriptor.

slots.meaning: The underside/undercroft beneath the monument.
slots.guidance: A short benign underside/undercroft descriptor.
